message:
bench: drop tokio comparison workloads from asupersync benchmarks

Continues the native-asupersync cleanup started by the earlier
refactor(core): remove direct tokio bench deps commit.  The criterion
benches in frankenterm-core were still importing tokio types and
running side-by-side tokio-vs-asupersync measurements purely for
historical comparison.  Now that the migration is complete, those
comparisons are noise: they slow every bench run, pin tokio into the
dev-dep graph, and constantly show up in the forbidden-tokens guard
output as if tokio were still a real runtime surface in the project.

Per bench:
- fts_query.rs: drop the tokio-mpsc fan-out baseline, keep the
  asupersync channel case.
- labruntime_overhead.rs: drop the tokio::runtime single-thread reference
  bench; LabRuntime overhead is what the perf budget now cares about.
- sizing_benchmark.rs: drop the tokio default multi-thread sizing curve;
  keep the asupersync sizing curves that drive the tuning playbook.
- spike_comparison.rs: remove all five tokio spike baselines
  (unix_pdu, two_phase_send, pool_pattern, sleep_wakeup, select_race)
  and their budget entries, retitle the module header.
- storage_regression.rs: drop the tokio-driven IO regression run.
- sync_primitives.rs: drop the tokio Mutex/Semaphore/mpsc baselines,
  keep the asupersync equivalents.
- task_spawn.rs: drop spawn-noop/region_batch/flat_fanout tokio
  baselines and the build_tokio_runtime helper; update the module
  comment to stop listing the comparison case.
- test_execution.rs: drop tokio JoinSet comparison.
- timer_precision.rs: drop the tokio::time::sleep precision reference
  so only the LabRuntime virtual-sleep measurement remains.

Side effects in the lockfile: criterion no longer depends on futures
or tokio in our workspace feature set, which shrinks the dev graph.

The two refreshed validation JSON files (asupersync-cutover-runtime-
guard-validation.json and ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-
validation.json) are simply the regenerated outputs from the
forbidden-token scan — the \`tokio::\` count drops from 201 to 181 and
the bench files fall off the "files" list, exactly matching the
benches being removed.
